mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance (p>0.05) during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in over 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE exceeds 8%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business confidence, driving strong positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences, or interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during periods of stable inflation reflects robust supply-side capacity utilization, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative during late-cycle expansions, or 5-day forward returns show zero correlation with INDPRO momentum in recovery regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth above 2.0 percent confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which translates into higher corporate revenues and positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC remains below 0.01, or GDPC1 growth rates above 2.0% consistently precede negative 60-day equity returns.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation breaching the 2.5 percent annual threshold forces expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, which increases discount rates and produces negative forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally by more than 1.5% over 20 days following CPIAUCSL threshold breaches in over 50% of cases, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls combined with expanding retail sales demonstrates income-driven consumption growth, creating a synergistic positive effect on corporate earnings and equity valuations.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS and RSAFS growth rates yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample testing, or 20-day returns underperform baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production alongside rising inflation indicates stagflationary supply constraints, which compress profit margins and trigger sustained negative equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive or neutral during confirmed stagflationary episodes, or the joint signal fails to separate from market noise in walk-forward validation.
